title: PSYC 231 Lecture 17 Liking and Love Quiz

questions.prompt: Which situation best illustrates the mere exposure effect?
options: A student becomes more attracted to a classmate after repeatedly seeing them in lectures
A student is attracted to someone only after learning they have similar values
A student falls in love after one intense conversation
A student likes someone because their friends approve of them

questions.prompt: Which finding is most consistent with lecture material on similarity and attraction?
options: Opposites usually attract most in long-term relationships
Similarity in attitudes and values tends to increase liking
Physical attractiveness matters only at the start of dating
People prefer partners with completely different self-esteem levels

questions.prompt: A person notices their partner is stressed, listens carefully, and responds in a way that makes the partner feel understood and cared for. This best reflects:
options: Social exchange
Companionate love
Responsiveness
Positive illusions

questions.prompt: According to Sternberg-style distinctions discussed in lecture, a relationship high in passion and intimacy but low in commitment would be best described as:
options: Romantic love
Companionate love
Fatuous love
Empty love

questions.prompt: Which person is most likely to remain in a relationship according to Social Exchange Theory?
options: Someone with low satisfaction, few barriers to leaving, and many attractive alternatives
Someone with high satisfaction, strong barriers to leaving, and few attractive alternatives
Someone with low commitment but many attractive alternatives
Someone whose only reason for staying is physical proximity

questions.prompt: A student insists that their partner is more thoughtful, funnier, and more considerate than everyone else thinks, and this belief is associated with high relationship satisfaction. This is most consistent with:
options: Stereotype threat
Negative reciprocity
Positive illusions
Destiny beliefs

questions.prompt: Which response to relationship distress is active and constructive?
options: Voice
Neglect
Exit
Loyalty

questions.prompt: A person believes that good relationships are built through effort, repair, and learning from conflict rather than being naturally perfect from the start. This best reflects:
options: Destiny beliefs
Growth beliefs
Positive illusions
Halo effect

questions.prompt: Which behaviour would Gottman most likely classify as contempt?
options: Telling a partner, 'I felt hurt when you ignored me at dinner'
Refusing to speak for 20 minutes to calm down and then returning to talk
Rolling one's eyes and mocking a partner during an argument
Disagreeing with a partner about finances

questions.prompt: Which statement is most supported by lecture material on relationship satisfaction over time?
options: Satisfaction always drops steadily and never improves
Communication, intimacy, and need fulfillment can help maintain relationships over time
Passion is the only factor that predicts long-term satisfaction
Once couples begin fighting, dissolution is inevitable
